_doc: Per-user partition spec, one entry per data-user (good + bad + freerider).
Activate by setting partition_strategy='per_user' + per_user_partitions='<this path>'
in ExperimentConfiguration.
Fields per user:
id         (int, required)        User Id
data_percent       (float, required)      Per-user budget as % of dataset.
Sum across users <= 100.
In overlap mode the per-user budget
is multiplied by replication_factor
(so total assigned ~ rep * dataset).
label_distribution (dict[int->float])     Optional per-class weights (relative;
normalized internally). Slices the
total budget across the listed labels.
only_labels        (list[int])            Optional whitelist. If set without
label_distribution, budget is split
stratified across the allowed classes.
flip_map           (dict[int->int])       Optional malicious label-flip map.
Wraps the dataset on read.
Per-class supply check is order-independent: total demand for each class
(summed across users) must not exceed dataset's class size (or class size *
replication_factor in overlap mode). Conflicts raise ValueError up front.
Skewed users (label_distribution / only_labels) eat extra share of their classes;
stratified users (no rules) consume each class proportionally to its size,
so heavy skew + sum=100 + rep>1 typically over-subscribes. Leave headroom by
lowering pcts on skewed users or reducing total sum below 100.
Example below: 8 data-users on MNIST (10 classes) tuned for replication_factor=2.
Sum=80 leaves headroom for the two skewed users; user 2 carries the malicious
flip_map. Verified per-class demand <= class_size * 2 across all 10 labels.
Remember to set number_of_good_contributors + number_of_bad_contributors +
number_of_freerider_contributors after adding or removing users in ExperimentConfiguration.
